message:
🌱 Add consul: [feature] HTTP/2 and gRPC health-checks support mission (#1203)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,76 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "consul-3073-feature-http-2-and-grpc-health-checks-support",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "consul: [feature] HTTP/2 and gRPC health-checks support",
+    "description": "[feature] HTTP/2 and gRPC health-checks support. Requested by 27+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current consul deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your consul version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul\nhelm list -n consul 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working consul installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review consul configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant consul configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul\nkubectl get configmap -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=consul\n```\nGiven the raising popularity of HTTP/2 and gRPC it'd be nice to be able to health-check such services natively with Consul."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for [feature] HTTP/2 and gRPC health-checks support",
+        "description": "This makes some minor tweaks to #3782 and adds documentation. I also renamed the `tls` config to `grpc_use_tls` to bind it closer to how it's used.\n```yaml\nfunc init() {\n\thttp.HandleFunc(\"/debug/requests\", func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n...\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul\nkubectl get events -n consul --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"[feature] HTTP/2 and gRPC health-checks support\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "This makes some minor tweaks to #3782 and adds documentation. I also renamed the `tls` config to `grpc_use_tls` to bind it closer to how it's used.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "func init() {\n\thttp.HandleFunc(\"/debug/requests\", func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n...",
+        "panic: http: multiple registrations for /debug/requests\n\ngoroutine 1 [running]:\nnet/http.(*ServeMux).Handle(0x1f7eb80, 0x14f8430, 0xf, 0x1e10100, 0x1544bd8)\n        /usr/lib/go/src/net/http/server.go:2270 +0x627\nnet/http.(*ServeMux).HandleFunc(0x1f7eb80, 0x14f8430, 0xf, 0x1544bd8)\n        /usr/lib/go/src/net/http/server.go:2302 +0x55\nnet/http.HandleFunc(0x14f8430, 0xf, 0x1544bd8)\n        /usr/lib/go/src/net/http/server.go:2314 +0x4b\ngithub.com/hashicorp/consul/vendor/github.com/hashicorp/go-discover/provider/gce/vendor/golang.org/x/net/trace.init.0()\n        /home/edio/development/go/src/github.com/hashicorp/consul/vendor/github.com/hashicorp/go-discover/provider/gce/vendor/golang.org/x/net/trace/trace.go:114 +0x42\ngithub.com/hashicorp/consul/vendor/github.com/hashicorp/go-discover/provide"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "consul",
+      "community",
+      "service-mesh",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "consul"
+    ],

+    ],

+      "issue": "https://github.com/hashicorp/consul/issues/3073",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/hashicorp/consul",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/hashicorp/consul/pull/3855"
+    },
+    "reactions": 27,
+    "comments": 11,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with consul installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
